description: Modal shape descriptions for all (m,n) index combinations from (1,1) to (6,6) for square plates, and (d,c) combinations for circular plates. Each entry describes the spatial pattern of the eigenmode — the zero-contour of the mode shape function.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Two diagonal nodal lines crossing at the plate center. Resembles the letter X or a saltire (diagonal cross). Four equal triangular antinodal quadrants radiate from the center intersection.
square_plate_modes.notes: The fundamental mode of a square plate. Easiest to excite with a bow.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: One nodal line parallel to the longer edge, plus one partial diagonal, creating an asymmetric T-bar or L-shape. Three nodal segments separate four unequal antinodal regions.
square_plate_modes.notes: Degenerate with (2,1). Orientation depends on bow position.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Mirror image of (1,2) mode. One nodal line parallel to the short edge plus one partial diagonal — the T-bar is rotated 90°. Same frequency as (1,2) but different spatial orientation.
square_plate_modes.notes: Degenerate with (1,2). Together these two modes produce diagonal patterns when superimposed.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Four nodal lines — two parallel to each edge — forming a hash-mark (#) or plus-sign (+) pattern. The two lines divide the plate into a 2×2 grid with a central square and corner lobes. Sometimes called the 'four-leaf clover' pattern.
square_plate_modes.notes: First four-fold symmetric mode above (1,1). Very photogenic.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Two nodal lines parallel to one plate edge (creating three parallel stripes), plus one nodal line parallel to the other edge. Results in a striped appearance with three vertical bands and a cross-bar.
square_plate_modes.visual: Three horizontal stripes with one vertical bar
square_plate_modes.notes: Degenerate with (3,1). Stripe patterns are among the most recognizable Chladni figures.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Two nodal lines parallel to one axis, one to the other — a 90° rotation of the (1,3) pattern. Three vertical stripe-like antinodal regions separated by two vertical lines, with a single horizontal crossbar.
square_plate_modes.visual: Three vertical stripes with one horizontal bar

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Five nodal lines — two in one direction and three in the other — creating a 2×3 grid of six elongated oval antinodal cells. The cells are arranged in two columns of three. The pattern has a clean rectangular lattice appearance.
square_plate_modes.visual: 2x3 rectangular lattice of six oval cells
square_plate_modes.notes: Degenerate with (3,2). Commonly photographed due to its clear lattice structure.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: 90° rotation of the (2,3) pattern. Six antinodal cells arranged in three rows of two — a 3×2 grid. The elongation axis of the cells is rotated compared to (2,3).
square_plate_modes.visual: 3x2 rectangular lattice of six oval cells
square_plate_modes.notes: Degenerate with (2,3). Superposition of (2,3) and (3,2) produces diagonal patterns.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Eight nodal lines — four in each direction — forming a dense checkerboard-like 4×4 grid. Sixteen antinodal cells arranged in a 4×4 matrix with alternating 'up' and 'down' vibration. Very regular and visually impressive.
square_plate_modes.notes: Second C4v-symmetric mode. One of the most photographed complex square plate modes.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Three nodal lines parallel to one edge and one parallel to the other, creating a four-stripe pattern in one direction. Eight antinodal cells arranged in one row of four pairs.
square_plate_modes.visual: Four parallel bands with one perpendicular bar
square_plate_modes.notes: Stripe pattern becoming more refined. Degenerate with (4,1).

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Rotated (1,4) pattern — four stripes running perpendicular to the previous mode. Three nodal lines along one axis, one along the other.
square_plate_modes.visual: Four vertical parallel bands with single horizontal bar

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Seven nodal lines — two in one direction, four in the other — creating a 2×4 grid of eight cells. The eight antinodal cells are elongated in the direction of fewer nodal lines.
square_plate_modes.visual: 2x4 grid of eight elongated oval cells
square_plate_modes.notes: Degenerate with (4,2). Produces elongated cell patterns.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Rotated (2,4) pattern with eight cells in a 4×2 arrangement. Four rows of two cells each, elongated in the perpendicular direction.
square_plate_modes.visual: 4x2 grid of eight elongated oval cells

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Ten nodal lines — three in one direction, four in the other — creating a 3×4 grid of twelve antinodal cells. The cells are slightly elongated in the direction of fewer nodal lines. Pattern requires lycopodium at high frequencies.
square_plate_modes.notes: Degenerate with (4,3). Transition to complex high-frequency patterns.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Rotated (3,4) pattern. Twelve cells in a 4×3 arrangement — the longer axis rotated 90°.
Twelve nodal lines — four in each direction — forming a dense 5×5 symmetric lattice with 25 antinodal cells. Very fine, regular pattern. All cells approximately equal in size and shape.
square_plate_modes.notes: Third C4v-symmetric mode. Requires very fine powder (lycopodium preferred).

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Four parallel nodal lines in one direction, one in the other — creating a five-stripe pattern with ten antinodal cells. The stripes are fine and delicate.
square_plate_modes.visual: Five parallel bands with single perpendicular bar

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Rotated (1,5) mode — five fine stripes running in the perpendicular direction.
square_plate_modes.visual: Five vertical parallel bands with single bar

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Nine nodal lines creating a 2×5 grid of ten cells. The cells appear as two long columns of five small cells each.
square_plate_modes.visual: 2x5 grid of ten narrow cells in two tall columns

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Rotated (2,5) mode. Ten cells in a 5×2 grid — five rows of two cells.
square_plate_modes.visual: 5x2 grid of ten cells in five wide rows

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Twelve nodal lines creating a 3×5 grid of 15 cells. The pattern fills the plate densely. Fine lycopodium required.
Rotated (3,5). Fifteen cells in a 5×3 arrangement.
Fourteen nodal lines creating a 4×5 grid of 20 cells. Very fine lattice visible only with lycopodium.
square_plate_modes.notes: High complexity mode approaching limits of dry powder visualization.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Rotated (4,5) — 20 cells in 5×4 arrangement.
Sixteen nodal lines — eight in each direction — forming a dense 6×6 symmetric lattice with 36 antinodal cells. Extremely fine pattern visible only with lycopodium and careful amplitude control.
square_plate_modes.notes: Fourth C4v mode. Very high complexity. Near-limit of dry particle visualization on standard plates.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Eleven nodal lines creating a 2×6 grid of 12 cells. Very elongated cells in two tall columns.
square_plate_modes.visual: 2x6 grid of twelve tall narrow cells

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Rotated (2,6) — 12 cells in 6×2 wide rows.
square_plate_modes.visual: 6x2 grid of twelve wide flat cells

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Fourteen nodal lines creating an 18-cell 3×6 rectangular lattice. Fine cells require lycopodium.
Rotated (3,6) — 18 cells in 6×3 wide grid.
Sixteen nodal lines creating a 4×6 grid of 24 antinodal cells. Dense rectangular lattice.
Rotated (4,6) — 24 cells in 6×4 arrangement.
Eighteen nodal lines creating a 5×6 grid of 30 antinodal cells. Extremely dense, requires very fine particles.
square_plate_modes.notes: Degenerate with (6,5). Approaching limits of standard visualization.

square_plate_modes.shape_description: Rotated (5,6) — 30 cells in 6×5 arrangement.
Twenty nodal lines — ten in each direction — forming a 7×7 symmetric lattice with 49 antinodal cells. Near-fractal complexity for a single vibrational mode. Extremely fine grain size required.
square_plate_modes.notes: Highest (m,m) symmetric mode catalogued. Dense and beautiful — a showcase of acoustic physics at the edge of visualization.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Breathing mode: single concentric nodal circle. The plate interior oscillates opposite in phase to the plate exterior. One perfect sand ring appears at the nodal radius (approximately 0.68R for free-edge plates).
circular_plate_modes.notes: Simplest circular plate mode showing full rotational symmetry. Very stable and easy to excite.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Diametral mode: a single straight nodal line passes through the plate center. The line can be oriented at any angle depending on initial conditions. Two semicircular antinodal regions vibrate in opposite phase.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Single diameter — bisects plate into two semicircles
circular_plate_modes.notes: Lowest-frequency mode on a free-edge circular plate. The simplest nodal line pattern.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Second breathing mode: two concentric nodal rings. Three concentric annular zones alternate in vibration phase. The inner ring is at ~0.38R, outer ring at ~0.84R. A target-like pattern.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Two nested concentric rings — three-zone target

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Two nodal diameters at 90° creating a cross or plus-sign pattern. Four equal pie-segment antinodal regions. The cross arms can be oriented as + or × depending on how the plate is excited.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Diameter cross (+) — four equal pie segments
circular_plate_modes.notes: Can appear as + or × depending on excitation orientation. Degenerate with rotated version.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: One nodal diameter plus one nodal circle. The circle is divided into four regions — two inner (within the ring) and two outer (outside the ring). The pattern resembles a yin-yang or divided circle.
circular_plate_modes.visual: One diameter plus one ring — four-region yin-yang
circular_plate_modes.notes: Mixed mode combining diameter and ring features.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Three nodal diameters at 60° creating a six-armed asterisk or snowflake. Six equal pie-segment antinodal zones. One of the most striking circular plate patterns.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Six-armed asterisk — six equal pie segments
circular_plate_modes.notes: First C6v symmetric mode. Often appears at concert A (440 Hz) on 20cm plates.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Three concentric nodal rings creating four alternating annular zones. The ring radii are at approximately 0.26R, 0.58R, and 0.88R. Four-zone bulls-eye pattern.
Two nodal diameters (cross) plus one nodal circle. Eight distinct antinodal regions — four inner quadrant segments and four outer arc segments. Cross-with-ring pattern.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Cross inside ring — eight distinct zones
circular_plate_modes.notes: Mixed-mode combining the 2-diameter cross and one ring.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Four nodal diameters at 45° creating an eight-armed star or wheel-spoke pattern. Eight equal pie-segment antinodal sectors. Very symmetric and photogenic.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Eight-spoked wheel — eight equal pie sectors
circular_plate_modes.notes: Appears visually like a mandala or compass rose.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: One nodal diameter plus two nodal circles. Six antinodal zones — three on each side of the diameter, each divided into inner, middle, and outer annular segments.
circular_plate_modes.visual: One diameter plus two rings — six zones
circular_plate_modes.notes: Complex mixed mode with bilateral symmetry.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Three nodal diameters plus one ring. Twelve distinct antinodal zones — six inner triangular segments within the ring, and six outer sectors outside the ring. Twelve-petal flower pattern.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Six-armed star with ring — twelve-zone flower
circular_plate_modes.notes: One of the most beautiful complex circular modes.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Five nodal diameters at 36° creating a ten-armed star with ten equal pie-segment antinodal sectors. Decagonal symmetry — rare in Chladni figures.
circular_plate_modes.notes: Decagonal symmetry is very rare in natural vibrating systems.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Four nodal diameters plus one ring. Sixteen distinct antinodal zones — eight inner quadrant-segments inside the ring, eight outer arc-sectors outside. Dense eight-fold pattern.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Eight-spoked wheel with ring — sixteen zones
circular_plate_modes.notes: High-symmetry mode appearing at higher frequencies.

circular_plate_modes.shape_description: Four concentric nodal rings creating five alternating annular zones. Dense rings spaced approximately equally across the plate radius. Five-zone bulls-eye.
Six nodal diameters at 30° creating a twelve-armed star with twelve equal pie sectors. The six-fold symmetry produces a pattern resembling a clock face or Chinese compass.
circular_plate_modes.visual: Twelve-armed star or clock face — dodecagonal symmetry
circular_plate_modes.notes: Twelve-fold symmetry rarely seen in physical demonstrations.
